parityMeta.describes: iOS-17e-6a — fineTune LIVE, projection APPLIED. A compound templateExercise (repMin 6 / repMax 8 / sets 3 / progressionUnitKg 5 / progressionPercent [5,10]) with three RECENT in-window push sessions at a rising top weight (50 -> 55 -> 60 kg, all 3x8 reps = hit ceiling) drives shouldAdd. With asOfDate = deterministicClockIso (the §11.2 injected clock), the LIVE buildSetWeightFineTune projects a +4%/wk-capped e1RM -> a 2.5kg-grid weight (62.5) that is MORE conservative than the blunt legacy +increment(5)=65: applyFineTuneIfDataRich clamps to ±10% of lastWeight(60)=[54,66], 2.5-rounds, the legacy-respect guard is EVALUATED but skipped (rounded 62.5 > lastWeight 60), and the projection is returned (62.5). This is the CONTRAST fixture: makeSuggestion(nil) would fall back to insufficient_history -> legacy baseline 65, so the live path provably CHANGES the output. FUNCTION-LEVEL compute-assert on the Swift side.
parityMeta.sourceNotes: asOfDate=true -> the generator passes deterministicClockIso as the injected fineTune clock, so the RECENT in-window history (daysAgo 4/11/18 spaced 7d -> distinct week indices 7/6/5) drives the live projection deterministically (NOT the wall clock). Sessions listed NEWEST-FIRST. Rising e1RM (epley 50/55/60 @8 = 63.33/69.67/76) -> positive slope, currentE1rm 76 capped to 79.04 -> weightForTarget(8) 62.4 -> 62.5. progressionUnitKg 5 makes the legacy increment 5 (baseline 65), so the conservative fineTune 62.5 diverges from legacy.
